test_series_name: Tax and Customs Theory 2026 - 12 Main Tests
notes: These tests are based on the uploaded Tax and Customs Theory 2026 presentation.
The sequence follows a progression from foundational recall to broader application and mixed-topic reasoning.
Time limits increase toward later tests.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid consecutive repetition of the same answer letter.

tests.title: Tax and Customs Theory 2026 - Overview of Customs - Main Test 1
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the presentation.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Who is obliged by law to submit inward and outward reports according to the slides?

options.A: The owner or agent of the means of conveyance
options.B: Only the WCO Secretariat
options.C: Only the Customs Co-operation Council
options.D: Only the passenger with personal baggage

questions.explanation: The inward/outward reports slide places the obligation on the owner or agent.

questions.question: How did taxation begin in early communities according to the presentation?

options.A: Only priests collected customs tariffs at temples
options.B: Merchants created a customs union before rulers intervened
options.C: Members of communities gave their chiefs or leaders gifts in return for leadership
options.D: Kings imposed VAT on all goods at the seaport

questions.explanation: The slides explain taxation began with gifts to chiefs or leaders.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? How many customs ports had been established in Canada by 1845?
questions.explanation: The presentation states that 63 customs ports in Canada had been established by 1845.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An administration needs guidance on HS classification, valuation, and rules of origin while modernizing customs systems. Which institution from the presentation is most relevant?

options.A: Only the 1643 English Board of Customs
options.B: The World Customs Organization
options.C: The Customs Preventive Service of Ghana
options.D: The EAC Mediation Agreement

questions.explanation: The WCO is presented as the key body for HS, valuation, rules of origin, and modernization support.

questions.question: Who must make declaration of goods according to the presentation?

options.A: Every importer in the inward report
options.B: Only members of the WCO Council
options.C: Only warehouse keepers
options.D: Only exporters using the red channel

questions.explanation: The customs declaration slide states every importer in the inward report must declare goods.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Which issue is specifically included under Customs protection of society and industry?

options.A: Only internal municipal licensing
options.B: Only warehouse rent disputes
options.C: Counterfeits and contrabands
options.D: Only school curriculum reform

questions.explanation: Counterfeits and contrabands appear on the protection slide.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which export-related goods are under customs control according to the presentation?

options.A: Only exports after final sale
options.B: Only exports that are never restricted
options.C: Only exports already cleared from customs control
options.D: All dutiable exports from when they are brought to a place for exportation until exportation

questions.explanation: The presentation lists dutiable exports in this way.

questions.question: When was the convention establishing the Customs Cooperation Council signed?

options.A: On 26 January 1953 in Paris
options.B: On 15 December 1950 in Brussels
options.C: On 4 November 1952 in London
options.D: In 1994 in Brussels

questions.explanation: The slides state the convention was signed on 15 December 1950 in Brussels.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is one of the stated objectives of the Tax and Customs Theory course?

options.A: To train only on tariff computation and valuation schedules
options.B: To replace all customs laws with administrative practice
options.C: To focus exclusively on East African ports and airports
options.D: To define Customs, explain the historical evolution of Customs, explain the evolution of modern Customs administrations, and discuss future roles of Customs administrations

questions.explanation: The objectives slide lists these four broad outcomes.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which enforcement-related items are specifically mentioned as being under customs control?

options.A: Only goods after import duty has been remitted
options.B: Only goods that are not declared anywhere
options.C: All seized goods and goods under notice of seizure
options.D: Only goods in private shops

questions.explanation: The slides list seized goods and goods under notice of seizure.

questions.question: Which is an example of a non-tariff barrier from the slides?

options.A: Gross weight duty calculation
options.B: Quota system
options.C: Common currency in 1949
options.D: WCO Secretariat staffing

questions.explanation: Quota system is listed among non-tariff barriers.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? When did international cooperation in customs matters begin under the League of Nations according to the slides?
questions.explanation: The development slide says cooperation began under the League of Nations in 1924.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which additional factor contributed to the collapse of the EAC?

options.A: Excessive use of the green channel
options.B: Continued disproportionate sharing of Community benefits among Partner States
options.C: Too much private sector participation
options.D: Overuse of non-intrusive inspection

questions.explanation: The presentation says disproportionate benefit sharing contributed to collapse.

questions.question: What was the purpose of the Board of Customs established by ordinance on 21 January 1643?

options.A: To draft the modern HS nomenclature
options.B: To unify the customs boards of England, Scotland, and Ireland immediately
options.C: To regulate, manage, and centralize the collection of duties on imported and some exported goods
options.D: To replace all English courts with customs courts

questions.explanation: The 1643 Board of Customs was meant to regulate, manage, and centralize duty collection.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Which combination correctly reflects features of East African customs in the presentation?

options.A: Full prohibition of internal trade; end of currency systems; no common administration
options.B: Only one port allowed per country; no common tariff; no free movement
options.C: Replacement of customs duties with only direct taxes
options.D: Free movement of goods, capital and labor; free trade in East African origin products; common administration of transport and communication

questions.explanation: These features are listed together on the presentation slides.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which is one of the WCO's stated objectives in the presentation?

options.A: To facilitate international trade through simplification and harmonization of customs procedures
options.B: To eliminate all document verification
options.C: To replace national customs administrations completely
options.D: To abolish all customs duties everywhere

questions.explanation: Trade facilitation through simplification and harmonization is one of the WCO objectives listed.

questions.question: How does the presentation describe EACCMA 2004?

options.A: As a body that replaced all regulations
options.B: As the principal law on Customs used in Kenya and EAC partner states that have joined the Customs Union
options.C: As a historical European customs treaty only
options.D: As a non-binding guideline for private traders

questions.explanation: The EACCMA slide calls it the principal law on Customs.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? When were English collectors of Customs first appointed?
questions.explanation: The presentation gives 1298 as the first appointment of English collectors of Customs.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which is one justification for customs barriers mentioned in the presentation?

options.A: Protection of infant industries
options.B: To eliminate all customs administrations
options.C: To end all health controls
options.D: To prevent all trade statistics collection

questions.explanation: The justification slide begins with protection of infant industries.

questions.question: What is a non-tariff barrier according to the presentation?

options.A: A customs duty computed on gross weight
options.B: A customs officer declaration
options.C: A court-issued warrant of distress
options.D: A condition or restriction imposed to regulate trade without being a direct tax

questions.explanation: The presentation distinguishes non-tariff barriers from tariff barriers.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Which activity is part of customs assessment and collection of taxes on imported goods?

options.A: Replacing the HS nomenclature
options.B: Judging all commercial disputes
options.C: Valuation of goods
options.D: Drafting national constitutions

questions.explanation: The assessment and collection slide lists valuation as one of the steps.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Who heads the WCO Secretariat?

options.A: The Chair of the EAC Council of Ministers
options.B: The Secretary General
options.C: The Commissioner
options.D: The Principal Collector

questions.question: How did taxation begin in early communities according to the presentation?

options.A: Kings imposed VAT on all goods at the seaport

options.D: Members of communities gave their chiefs or leaders gifts in return for leadership

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Which is one basic control tool used by Customs according to the presentation?

options.A: Appointment and gazettement of entry and exit points such as seaports, airports, and border stations
options.B: Removal of all mandatory declarations
options.C: Abolition of customs areas
options.D: Ending control of conveyances

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the basic rule on loading or unloading cargo before entry?

options.A: Cargo may be loaded before entry if trade statistics have been collected
options.B: Only vessel cargo must wait, but aircraft cargo need not
options.C: Loading is always allowed before entry if the goods are valuable
options.D: No unloading or loading of cargo prior to entry

questions.explanation: The customs declaration slide clearly states no unloading or loading prior to entry.

questions.question: When was the convention establishing the Customs Cooperation Council signed?

options.A: On 26 January 1953 in Paris
options.B: On 15 December 1950 in Brussels
options.C: On 4 November 1952 in London
options.D: In 1994 in Brussels

questions.explanation: The slides state the convention was signed on 15 December 1950 in Brussels.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Which is listed as one of the key legal authorities for customs work?

options.C: English Board of Customs Ordinance, 1643

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which facilitation incentives or arrangements are mentioned in the presentation?

options.A: Only anti-dumping duties and countervailing duties

options.C: SEZ, EPZ, EPAs, AfCFTA, AGOA, EAC, and COMESA

questions.question: What are the working languages of the WCO according to the presentation?

options.A: English and Portuguese
options.B: French and Swahili
options.C: English and Arabic
options.D: English and French

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? When did the centralized English Customs system begin according to the presentation?

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which is a key feature of East African customs mentioned in the presentation?

options.A: No role for customs statistics
options.B: No movement of goods within East Africa
options.C: Single collection center of import duty at the point of entry
options.D: Mandatory abolition of all border stations

questions.question: Which is a key feature of East African customs mentioned in the presentation?

options.A: No role for customs statistics

options.C: Mandatory abolition of all border stations

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Which is one justification for customs barriers mentioned in the presentation?

options.A: To prevent all trade statistics collection

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? By 1900, what scale had the Chinese customs system reached according to the presentation?

options.A: 151 staff in a single secretariat in Brussels

questions.question: What is the stated rule on unloading before an inward report for imports?

options.A: There should be no unloading before the report except with permission of Customs

options.C: Only aircraft must wait for reporting, not vessels
options.D: Unloading is always allowed before any report

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is a non-tariff barrier according to the presentation?

options.A: A court-issued warrant of distress

options.C: A customs duty computed on gross weight

questions.question: What began in East Africa in 1923 according to the presentation?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Why did customs duties become reliable sources of public revenue in earlier times?

options.A: Because they were relatively easy to collect at specified points such as bridges and gates
options.B: Because customs applied only to luxury goods
options.C: Because customs officers worked without rulers
options.D: Because all internal taxes had already been abolished

questions.explanation: The presentation says customs duties were easy to collect and became reliable sources of public revenue.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A customs administration speeds up low-risk trade flows while still checking prohibited goods, collecting taxes, and protecting society. Which idea from the presentation best explains this balance?

options.A: Modern Customs should eliminate all restrictions and taxes at once
options.B: Customs exists only to run courts and not borders
options.C: Customs should only collect trade statistics and avoid control functions

questions.question: Which EACCMA section is linked to goods under customs control in the presentation?

options.B: Section 34
options.C: Section 229
options.D: Section 2

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which customs revenue issue contributed to the collapse of the EAC according to the presentation?

options.A: Use of baggage halls and transit sheds

options.C: Adoption of smart seals and drones
options.D: Failure to create the WTO

questions.question: What is the role of the EAC Customs Management Regulations according to the presentation?

options.A: They only apply to non-tariff barriers in Europe
options.B: They replace the Act entirely
options.C: They explain the Act further and provide details required for customs procedures and activities
options.D: They abolish schedules

questions.explanation: The EACCMR slide says regulations explain the Act and provide details.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Why does Customs collect statistics according to the presentation?

options.A: Only to determine judicial appeals
options.B: Only to support private warehouses
options.C: Only to punish traders

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? When was the convention establishing the Customs Cooperation Council signed?

options.A: On 4 November 1952 in London
options.B: On 15 December 1950 in Brussels

options.D: In 1994 in Brussels

questions.question: Which additional factor contributed to the collapse of the EAC?

options.A: Excessive use of the green channel

options.C: Continued disproportionate sharing of Community benefits among Partner States
options.D: Too much private sector participation

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Which of the following is listed as a customs area or control point in the presentation?

options.A: Baggage halls
options.B: Private residences by default
options.C: Parliamentary chambers
options.D: Company boardrooms

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? By 1900, what scale had the Chinese customs system reached according to the presentation?

questions.question: What problem in the period after the First World War pushed countries and traders toward customs cooperation?

options.A: Border controls were fully removed worldwide
options.B: Countries became highly protective, imposed high tariffs, and introduced complex customs procedures
options.C: All tariffs disappeared and customs lost relevance
options.D: Trade was regulated only by private contracts

questions.explanation: The slides describe post-war protectionism and procedural complexity.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is customs control according to the presentation?

options.A: Measures put in place to ensure compliance with customs laws and regulate movement of resources from, into, and within a country
options.B: The abolition of all border procedures
options.C: A system used only for customs statistics
options.D: The process of collecting only direct taxes on income

questions.explanation: This is the definition given on the customs control slide.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? When were English collectors of Customs first appointed?
questions.explanation: The presentation gives 1298 as the first appointment of English collectors of Customs.

questions.question: What important UK customs reform took place in 1823?

options.B: All customs duties were repealed
options.C: The WCO Secretariat moved to London
options.D: The separate boards of England, Scotland, and Ireland were merged into one unified Board of Customs

questions.explanation: The UK revolutionized customs management in 1823 by merging separate customs boards.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What are customs barriers according to the slides?

options.C: Physical or non-physical structures employed to regulate movement of resources
options.D: Only judicial orders from tribunals
